Dashboard Archive: 2026-06-18 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's data shows two exceptionally strong momentum performers at the top of the ranker. SYRE leads decisively with a rank-1 score of 94.5, driven by extraordinary long-term returns (481.95% over 1 year) and perfect consistency across all price-moving average gates, though its extreme 1-year outperformance versus SPY (+456.98%) and QQQ (+441.97%) suggests it may be a highly concentrated or volatile position. AGX follows as the second-ranked ticker with a score in the low 90s, posting solid 1-year returns of 247.17% but notably with a weaker 1-month ratio (0.84) that breaks the consistency pattern—indicating momentum may be cooling compared to intermediate timeframes. Both tickers maintain all four SMA gates (trading above the 30, 50, 100, and 200-day moving averages), which is the baseline requirement for inclusion in the top tier, and both show strong resilience scores on down-SPY days. The data suggests a market environment where a small set of high-momentum names are pulling significantly away from broader indices, with SYRE's extraordinary 481% annual return being an outlier even among top performers.
